Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Inside The Airbus A350's Avionics Bay

The aircraft is one of the world's most advanced machines.

The A350's avionics architecture leverages modular, software-defined systems that significantly reduce maintenance complexity and enable real-time fleet health monitoring compared to legacy aircraft.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members

March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...

The CSF's expansion to include lunar mobility and advanced power solutions providers signals industry preparation for sustained commercial operations beyond Earth orbit.

See SpaceNews →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

Put Them On Blast: TMZ Asks Public For Photos Of Vacationing Congress Members Amid TSA Crisis

The shutdown, now in its 41st day, has left around 50,000 TSA officers without a paycheck.

The political spotlight on furloughed TSA operations could accelerate congressional budget resolution, directly impacting airport security screening delays and crew scheduling reliability.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...

Europe's delayed Gateway funding decision reflects broader uncertainty about NASA's shifting Artemis architecture and the viability of lunar orbit staging concepts.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

American Airlines Strongly Considers Bringing Back Seatback Screens, Eyes Starlink

American Airlines may bring back seatback screens while exploring Starlink Wi-Fi, signaling a shift toward enhanced inflight connectivity.

American Airlines' consideration of seatback screens alongside Starlink integration suggests carriers are betting that premium connectivity justifies the weight and maintenance costs of individual displays.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS transforms aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

Military Mar 27, 2026

Gallery: Luke Days 2026

A glimpse back at last weekend's air show at Luke Air Force Base in Arizona.

Luke Days showcases F-35 operations and training capabilities, offering insight into how the USAF's primary fighter platform integrates with civilian aviation communities.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching

Investigators say an instructional flight ended after a loss of oil pressure and total engine failure.

Total engine failure from oil pressure loss highlights critical importance of pre-flight fluid system inspections and engine monitoring protocols for instructional operations.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

How Premium Economy Has Changed Over The Last 10 Years

The transformation of premium economy from a niche offering to a central pillar of airline strategy has reshaped how passengers travel.

Premium economy's evolution from niche to core revenue driver reflects airlines' shift toward filling the middle-seat profitability gap between economy and business classes.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch

Technology and science demonstrations, supported by various NASA industry collaborations and agency developments, are set to launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the company’s Transporter-16 commercial rideshare mission. These demonstrations will test thermal...

NASA's use of commercial rideshare missions reduces launch costs while validating emerging technologies through industry partnerships, establishing a scalable model for frequent orbital testing.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management Systems meet FAA standards, creating legal accountability for their safety governance structure.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science

NASA has selected 10 participating scientists to help shape a science plan for astronauts to complete on the lunar surface under the Artemis program – including deploying scientific instruments, making critical observations of the landing site, and collecting Moon rocks. “Congratulations to the...

Artemis lunar missions will leverage scientist-astronaut collaboration to systematically map the south pole's geology and resources, directly informing future long-term human habitation strategies.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Three killed in Hawaii tour helicopter crash on Kauai

Three people died and two others were injured after an Airborne Aviation tour helicopter crashed on the afternoon…...

Tour helicopter operations face persistent safety challenges despite regulatory oversight, suggesting systemic vulnerabilities in how sightseeing flights manage risk in challenging terrain.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman

Listen to this audio excerpt from Michael Guzman, Artemis II main propulsion systems engineer: A clue to what Mike Guzman, main propulsion systems engineer at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, loves most can be found in the signature of his work email: a complex string of equations for rocket...

NASA's Artemis II propulsion engineer exemplifies how specialized expertise in rocket systems engineering drives deep-space exploration capabilities.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

SES meoSphere will be core to new MEO/LEO inflight connectivity

WASHINGTON D.C. — Rapidly putting the wheels in motion to launch a next-generation Medium Earth Orbit satellite network, dubbed meoSphere, SES intends to pair the new service with Low Earth Orbit satcom to power multi-orbit MEO/LEO inflight connectivity for airlines, company CEO Adel Al-Saleh...

SES's dual MEO/LEO satellite architecture addresses current capacity constraints by combining orbital layers for more reliable, higher-bandwidth inflight connectivity than single-orbit solutions.

See Runway Girl Network →

▶
General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model supporting both flight training and commercial operations demonstrates viable diversification strategy for mid-size regional airports beyond pure cargo or passenger focus.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Drones Mar 27, 2026

This firmware update makes Avata 360 ready for real flying

The new DJI Avata 360 drone is arriving with a feature-rich day-one firmware update that significantly expands what this immersive FPV drone can do right out of the box. The initial firmware rollout, headlined by v01.00.0100 for the aircraft, arrives alongside updates for the entire ecosystem,...

DJI's day-one firmware update for Avata 360 indicates manufacturers are addressing FPV drone operational limitations at launch, suggesting market demand for enhanced immersive flight capabilities.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 27, 2026

The World’s Largest Air Forces By Number Of Pilots

Which air force has the deepest roster?

Pilot inventory directly correlates with operational readiness and sustained combat capability, making workforce depth a critical competitive metric beyond aircraft fleet size.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive

India-based Bellatrix Aerospace announced March 27 it has raised $20 million to ramp up production of its satellite propulsion systems after securing its first large commercial customer outside the country.

Bellatrix's overseas customer acquisition validates India's indigenous satellite propulsion technology, positioning the nation as a competitive alternative to established Western aerospace suppliers.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad

Synopsis | 03/23/26 https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/e33cd0cc61064a6497a55fca8e9b30c6/view NASA intends to release a BAA under Next Space Technologies for Exploration Partnerships (NextSTEP-3), Appendix E, for Project NEXUS, Ka-band Backward- Compatible Relay. As the aging Tracking and Data...

NASA's NEXUS Ka-band relay system will extend satellite communication coverage while maintaining backward compatibility with existing ground infrastructure, reducing costly upgrades for aerospace operators.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

Reno-Tahoe pilots must carefully execute the Mustang 9 Departure from runways 17L/R due to critical procedural requirements that could impact safety and traffic flow.

Military Mar 27, 2026

The 5 Highest Altitude Reconnaissance Aircraft Currently Operated By The United States

These aircraft offer long-range capabilities, a factor that pairs well with their high-altitude flying.

High-altitude reconnaissance aircraft's extended range capability enables persistent surveillance over vast geographic areas with minimal refueling requirements.

Drones Mar 27, 2026

DJI Fly update adds support for new Avata 360 drone

The DJI Fly app has officially been updated to version 1.20.0, adding support for the newly launched Avata 360 drone. The update also confirms Avata 360’s compatibility with the DJI RC 2, DJI Goggles 3, and DJI Goggles N3. more…...

DJI's Avata 360 integration with existing RC and goggle hardware suggests the company is prioritizing ecosystem compatibility to reduce consumer friction and enable broader FPV drone adoption.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The $2B blind spot: Why ‘good enough’ is killing airline profitability

AeroTime columnist Ann Cederhall is an instructor with IATA on Airline Distribution Strategy and with Aeroclass on Airline Retailing, Ann is a frequent speaker and…...

Airlines' tolerance for suboptimal distribution and retailing practices costs $2 billion annually, suggesting significant margin recovery potential through systematic process improvement rather than revenue growth alone.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

Tennessee Airport Boards Face Possible State Takeover

Senate panel advances measure to replace existing airport authority boards with state appointees.

State-appointed governance could reshape operational autonomy and funding priorities for regional airports currently managed by local authorities.

See AVweb →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across the entire aviation system—not just aircraft technology—represents the most immediately scalable pathway to environmental sustainability amid projected traffic growth.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Artemis II Crew’s Suits

The Orion Crew Survival System suits that Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialist Christina Koch from NASA, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen from the CSA (Canadian Space Agency) will wear on the Artemis II test flight are seen in the suit-up room of the Neil A....

NASA's advanced Orion spacesuits represent a critical life-support evolution for lunar missions, setting performance standards that will influence future deep-space vehicle design requirements.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

EASA Warns Of Stolen Aircraft Parts Threatening Global Flights

The agency has been alerted to a compromised shipment of more than 600 parts from four engine families.

Compromised engine parts from a single shipment could affect multiple aircraft types globally, requiring operators to verify part provenance across their fleets.

Northrop Grumman, US Air Force begin Sentinel ICBM silo prototyping in Utah

The US Air Force and Northrop Grumman broke ground on a prototype launch silo for the LGM-35A Sentinel…...

The Sentinel program's silo prototype development marks the transition from design to physical validation, critical for meeting the Air Force's timeline to replace aging Minuteman III missiles by decade's end.

Flight School Closure Leaves Students Seeking Refunds

Students and another Missouri flight school dispute how unused training balances will be handled.

Flight school closures expose regulatory gaps in consumer protection for prepaid aviation training, creating precedent for how the industry must safeguard student investments.

Surprise! The US Airport With The World's Rarest Airbus A380 Service

The major US hub will have just 89 A380 departures this year.

Limited A380 service suggests carriers are consolidating wide-body operations on transcontinental routes rather than maintaining multiple domestic hub connections.

Boeing's 737 MAX 10 Certification: The Unseen Human Effort To Restore Trust

Testing the uncertified aircraft involves a huge team that stretches way beyond the pilots flying the aircraft.

Boeing's MAX 10 certification requires coordinated expertise across engineering, test, and regulatory teams whose behind-the-scenes validation work is critical to rebuilding industry and public confidence.

Video: F-18 Intercept Near Iran & Atlantic Engine Trouble with Sam Rutherford

Single-engine ferry pilot Sam Rutherford discusses everything from engine troubles over the Atlantic to an F-18 intercept near Iran.

Ferry pilots operating single-engine aircraft face critical safety decisions when engine issues develop over remote regions, requiring specialized risk assessment skills that differ significantly from typical commercial operations.
